title: taraxacum limbatum f. psammophilum
intro: Taraxacum limbatum f. psammophilum, also known as the psammophilous dandelion, is a subspecies of the Taraxacum limbatum plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It is primarily found in dry, sandy areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The psammophilous dandelion is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum limbatum f. psammophilum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
